title: TCP/IP Transport Layer
description: Provides end-to-end communication between applications using port numbers — TCP for reliability, UDP for speed.
fullExplanation: The TCP/IP Transport layer maps directly to OSI Layer 4. It provides end-to-end communication between applications on different hosts using port numbers to identify which application should receive data. TCP (Transmission Control Protocol) provides reliable, ordered, error-checked delivery with flow control and congestion control. UDP (User Datagram Protocol) provides fast, connectionless delivery with minimal overhead. SCTP combines features of both for telephony signaling.

protocols.name: TCP
protocols.description: Reliable, ordered, connection-oriented. 3-way handshake. Used by HTTP, SSH, SMTP.

protocols.name: UDP
protocols.description: Fast, connectionless, no guarantees. Used by DNS, DHCP, NTP, VoIP.

protocols.name: SCTP
protocols.description: Stream Control Transmission Protocol — multi-homing, multi-streaming. Used in telecom.

protocols.name: DCCP
protocols.description: Datagram Congestion Control Protocol — UDP with congestion control.

protocols.name: QUIC
protocols.description: Quick UDP Internet Connections — reliable streams over UDP. Used by HTTP/3.

keyFacts: Port numbers (0-65535) identify applications — multiplexing multiple apps over one IP
TCP: reliable, ordered, connection-oriented — 3-way handshake before data
UDP: connectionless, no guarantees — 8-byte header, minimal overhead
QUIC: modern replacement for TCP in HTTP/3 — runs over UDP
Transport layer PDU: Segment (TCP) or Datagram (UDP)
Well-known ports 0-1023 reserved for standard services (root required to bind)
